OPUS AUDIO DECODER INTEGRATION GUIDE
=====================================

CURRENT STATUS:
- Child side: Complete (AudioPlaybackCapture → Opus encode → DataChannel)
- Parent side: Infrastructure ready, decoder needs integration

PRODUCTION DECODER OPTIONS:

1. opus-decoder (Recommended)
   - WebAssembly-based Opus decoder
   - npm: @wasm-audio-decoders/opus
   - Usage:
     ```javascript
     import { OpusDecoder } from '@wasm-audio-decoders/opus';
     const decoder = new OpusDecoder();
     await decoder.ready;
     const pcmData = decoder.decode(opusPacket);
     ```

2. libopus.js
   - JavaScript port of libopus
   - GitHub: kazuki/opus.js-sample
   - Larger bundle size but no WASM dependency

3. MediaSource API (Fallback)
   - Wrap Opus in WebM container
   - Use MediaSource for playback
   - Higher latency but native browser support

INTEGRATION STEPS:

1. Install decoder library:
   npm install @wasm-audio-decoders/opus

2. Update opus-audio-player.js:
   - Import OpusDecoder
   - Initialize in init() method
   - Decode packets in decodeAndPlayOpusPacket()
   - Schedule PCM buffers via scheduleAudioBuffer()

3. Test with child device:
   - Start screen recording
   - Verify audio packets arrive
   - Confirm audio playback

CURRENT IMPLEMENTATION:
- Packets are received and logged
- Audio infrastructure is ready
- Only decoder integration is pending

AUDIO FORMAT:
- Sample rate: 48000 Hz
- Channels: 1 (Mono)
- Frame size: 20ms (960 samples)
- Bitrate: 32 kbps
- Encoding: Opus
